untranslatable_reason: Signal requires explicit 'implied volatility shrinking' condition. The allowed indicators include hvol_20 (historical volatility), but not implied volatility (IV). The entry condition comparing hvol_20 to itself is a placeholder and cannot properly express 'IV shrinks while stock rises' without access to an IV indicator. Additionally, the context-dependent nature of this signal (profit outlook improves vs. losses worsen based on stock direction) requires conditional logic beyond the supported operators.
translation_notes: This signal describes a volatility-based option spread strategy that cannot be fully translated without access to implied volatility indicators. The rule is fundamentally tied to options pricing dynamics (bull spread P&L sensitivity to IV changes) which are outside the scope of price-action and historical volatility indicators. A proper backtest would require iv_index or similar IV tracking indicator.
